preamble: Retraction from palatal to uvular nasals typically occurs in specific phonological environments, such as assimilation to back vowels or as a stage in systemic nasal harmony within certain phonetic structures.

examples.note: The transition from a palatal to a uvular articulation can be found in the historical development of nasal harmony in several African and South American languages where vowels influence the point of nasal articulation.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
